LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DR. KONSTANTIN BUKOV AND DR. EDWIN HASSID'S MOTION TO STRIKE EXEMPLARY DAMAGE CLAIMS IN PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT PLEAD A VIABLE ELDER ABUSE CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST THESE DEFENDANTS. THE COURT THEREFORE STRIKES THE EXEMPLARY DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AND GIVES PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT AN ELDER ABUSE CLAIM. THE EXEMPLARY ALLEGATIONS ARE IMPROPER BASED SOLELY ON A PROFESSIONAL MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION WHERE THERE IS NO COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 425.13. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

RULING - A R G U E D; The court vacated its tentative ruling: Defendant Konstantin Bukov's motion to strike portions of the second amended complaint is denied. There is no fraud claim alleged. All other allegations sought to be stricken are adequately alleged. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-28-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTOPHER DOLAN & ATTY. MEGAN IRIS OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-421-2800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BRYCE GRAY OF THE LAW OFFICES OF RANKIN, SPROATE, MIRES & MINTZ, TEL. NO. 1-510-465-3922 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT DR. BUKOV.

RULING - Judge Kahn disclosed that his father was at the Jewish Home for a short stint and possibly met a few times Mark Friedlander. This does not affect the ability of the judge to be fair and partial. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KONSTANTIN BUKOV's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Konstantin Bukov's demurrer to the first and second cause of action alleged in the second amended complaint is overruled as to both causes of action. Plaintiff Mattie Duncan may allege separate causes of action for elder abuse and elder neglect even though the concepts overlap in certain circumstances. (CACI 3103 and 3106). Ms. Duncan alleges sufficient facts showing that Dr. Bukov had a substantial caretaking or custodial relationship with regard to her basic needs to support the neglect claim. (Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 406-407; Winn v. Pioneer Medical Group, Inc. (2016) 63 Cal.4th 148, 158; Delaney v. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23, 34; second amended complaint pars. 21-25, 48, and 52-68.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Exemplary Damage Claims In Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Dr. Konstantin Bukov and Dr. Edwin Hassid's Motion To Strike Exemplary Damage Claims In Plaintiffs Complaint is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff does not plead a viable elder abuse cause of action against these defendants. The court therefore strikes the exemplary damages allegations and gives plaintiff leave to amend to allege facts to support an elder abuse claim. The exemplary allegations are improper based solely on a professional medical negligence cause of action where there is no compliance with CCP 425.13. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Konstanin Bukov and Edward Hassid's demurrer to the second cause of action in the complaint for elder abuse is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. To state a claim for elder abuse, a plaintiff is obligated to plead, inter alia, that a defendant was responsible for meeting the basic needs of the plaintiff and those allegations must be made with particularity. (Carter v. Prime Healthcare Paradise Valley LLC (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 406-407.) Paragraphs 3 and 48 of the complaint indicate that the Jewish Home was the entity responsible for meeting the basic needs of plaintiff Mattie Duncan. Liberally construed, paragraphs 13 and 15 allege that Dr. Bukov and Dr. Hassid were acting as plaintiff's treating physicians. Covenant Care v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 32 Cal.4th 771, 783 explains that, for purposes of the Elder Abuse Act, "neglect refers not to the substandard performance of medical services but, rather, to the 'failure of those responsible for attending to the basic needs and comforts of elderly or dependent adults, regardless of their professional standing, to carry out their custodial obligations.'" Ms. Duncan does not allege sufficient particular facts showing that Dr. Bukov and Dr. Hassid had a substantial caretaking or custodial relationship with regard to her basic needs. (Winn v. Pioneer Medical Group, Inc. (2016) 63 Cal.4th 148, 158.) At paragraphs 21 and 28, the complaint lumps all of the defendants together and conclusorily alleges that they were all responsible for plaintiff's basic needs. Those allegations are contrary to the requirements of Carter, 198 Cal.App.4th 396, 410. Plaintiff's reliance on Delaney v. Baker (1999) 20 Cal.4th 23, a post trial case, is misplaced. There, the elder resided at a skilled nursing facility where she had been left lying in her own urine and feces for extended periods of time because the defendants, upon whom she had relied to provide basic care, had failed to carry out their caretaking and custodial obligations. In this case, Ms. Duncan does not allege particular facts showing that Dr. Bukov and Dr. Hassid assumed such caretaking obligations. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
